vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical reality. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) is present, identified by the white rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (No visible plant matter in yellow pot)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No visible plant matter in black pot)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible plant matter in black pot)

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Pothos specimen remains; white rabbit anchor is present. Soil appears dark and moist.
plant_audit.P4_Black_Pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or perlite) on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Introduction of a metallic object (spoon) in T-3 and a white cylindrical object (likely a marker or sensor) in T-1 and CURRENT.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-4: Minimal change; pots appear largely devoid of foliage.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of a spoon into the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Removal of the spoon; lighting shift suggests camera adjustment.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Introduction of a white cylindrical object near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Stable state; no further biological growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total systemic failure regarding the registered flora. P3 is the only surviving specimen. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total senescence, removal, or burial.
anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical sensor/marker (T-1).
Presence of white granular debris (eggshells) in P3.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a desolate botanical environment. The registry is largely unsupported by the visual evidence, with P1, P2, and P4 showing no signs of life. The Pothos (P3) remains the sole occupant. The introduction of non-botanical items (spoon, sensor) suggests human intervention or experimental monitoring, but these have not resulted in any visible plant recovery or new growth.
